steps.title: Identify the relationship between the sentences
steps.content: The first two sentences describe a pattern: several female wood ducks visit dozens of nesting sites and lay eggs for others to incubate. Subject 8J21C, by contrast, visited only a select few nests and incubated her own eggs. The blank must introduce a contrasting behavior, not an example or similarity.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice by what it signals
steps.content:
The blank must signal that Subject 8J21C behaves differently from the group pattern. Test each option:
  • Choice A — for example,: signals that Subject 8J21C is an instance of the general pattern — but she behaves opposite to the pattern. ✗
  • Choice B — in particular,: singles out a specific member to illustrate the same pattern — again requires similarity, not contrast. ✗
  • Choice C — similarly,: signals that Subject 8J21C acts the same as the others — she does the opposite. ✗
  • Choice D — alternatively,: signals a contrasting option, showing that Subject 8J21C follows a different course from the pattern just described. ✓
That is the correct answer.
